description: Add the missing execution layer to the OpenClawd multi-agent platform: a Python supervisor daemon that monitors the coordination database, matches tasks to specialist agents, spawns parallel LLM API calls with agent-specific system prompts via a provider-agnostic abstraction layer, and flows structured results back into the existing coordination dashboard.

forbiddenPatterns: Direct imports from orchestrator-dashboard modules outside openclawd_adapter.py - use adapter isolation layer
Hardcoded database paths - resolve from config with fallback chain
String interpolation for SQL with agent-sourced data - always use parameterized queries
Fire-and-forget async operations - always await promises
Modifying existing OpenClawd tables or columns - only add new tables/columns
componentContext.discoveryNote: This is a Python backend system (not Next.js). No UI components are needed. All integration with the existing OpenClawd orchestrator-dashboard must go through the openclawd_adapter.py isolation layer.

systems.name: OrchestratorDashboard
systems.description: Existing Python module providing task CRUD, notifications, activity logging, squad chat, and agent coordination. Located at orchestrator-dashboard/dashboard.py

integration_points.create_task: Creates new task with domain, priority, assigned_agent
integration_points.complete_task: Marks task completed and internally creates notification + activity log
integration_points.add_task_contribution: Records deliverable and sets tasks.status='in_progress' as side effect
integration_points.squad_chat_post: Posts to team chat with optional task linkage

gotchas: OrchestratorDashboard.__init__ takes db_path defaulting to relative path - must resolve to absolute path
Each adapter method opens its own sqlite3.connect() and CANNOT participate in dispatch system transactions
complete_task() internally calls create_notification() and log_agent_activity() - do not send duplicate completion notifications
add_task_contribution() has side effect: sets tasks.status='in_progress' if current status is 'open'
tasks table has CHECK constraint on status limited to ('open','in_progress','review','completed','blocked') - dispatch system adds dispatch_status as separate column

systems.name: AgentCoordinator
systems.description: Keyword-based agent routing and domain-expertise matching for task assignment. Located at orchestrator-dashboard/agent_coordinator.py

integration_points._determine_relevant_agents: Scans task title+description for keywords, returns list of agent names
integration_points._can_agent_contribute: Checks if agent's expertise domains match task domain using agent_expertise dict
integration_points.agent_expertise: Dict mapping agent names to domain lists (rex: research/market_analysis, pixel: digital_products, haven: real_estate, vault: business_acquisition, nora: operations, scout: validation, keeper: maintenance)

gotchas: AgentCoordinator.__init__() takes no parameters and internally creates OrchestratorDashboard with default relative path - adapter must monkey-patch with correct instance
_determine_relevant_agents is private method (underscore prefix) - wrap defensively
Keyword matching is deterministic (no ML) based on inline keyword lists

systems.name: coordination.db
systems.description: Existing SQLite database with tasks, agent_activity, task_contributions, notifications, squad_chat, agent_checkins tables. Dispatch system extends with new tables and columns.

integration_points.tasks: Extended with dispatch_status and lease_until columns
integration_points.new_tables: dispatch_runs, task_dependencies, daily_usage, provider_health, provider_incidents, working_memory, audit_log, tasks_archive, health_daily_summary

gotchas: Existing schema does NOT use WAL mode - must set per-connection
Must set PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON per-connection (SQLite disables by default)
Never modify or drop existing OpenClawd tables or columns
Check for network filesystem (NFS/SMB) and warn about WAL unreliability

systems.name: skills
systems.description: 44 skill directories providing methodology/reference content for agents. Each has SKILL.md with description. Skills are read-only knowledge sources, not callable tools.

integration_points.SKILL.md: Read description/summary and inject into agent prompts
integration_points.AGENT_SKILLS: Mapping in config.py defines which skills each agent can access

gotchas: Verify no hardcoded user-specific paths remain in skills/self-evolving-skill/ at implementation time
Some skill directories may lack SKILL.md - handle missing files gracefully
Skills directory is sibling of orchestrator-dashboard/, not inside agent-dispatch/

systemContext.discoveryNote: All system integrations must go through openclawd_adapter.py isolation layer. Search orchestrator-dashboard/ for additional methods and patterns not explicitly documented.

Checks pricing_updated_at and logs warning if stale.
acceptanceCriteria: Function reads config['pricing'][provider][model] for input_per_1m_tokens and output_per_1m_tokens
Calculates cost as (input_tokens * input_price + output_tokens * output_price) / 1000000
Returns cost_estimate in USD as float
Checks config['pricing_updated_at'] and logs warning if date is > 90 days ago
Raises error if pricing not configured for provider/model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement data retention archival job
userStories.description: As the retention system, I want a daily archival job that copies completed tasks older than 90 days to tasks_archive, deletes from tasks (cascading to related tables), deletes output files, using explicit INSERT OR IGNORE / DELETE SQL, so that database stays manageable. **Implementation:** Function run_retention_cleanup(config, conn) runs archival SQL (INSERT OR IGNORE into tasks_archive, DELETE from tasks with id IN subquery), deletes output files for archived task IDs, aggregates health checks older than 30 days to health_daily_summary and deletes raw records, deletes agent_activity/dispatch_runs/working_memory older than 90 days, retains provider_incidents for 180 days.
acceptanceCriteria: Executes INSERT OR IGNORE INTO tasks_archive SELECT ... FROM tasks WHERE status='completed' AND updated_at < datetime('now','-90 days')
Executes DELETE FROM tasks WHERE status='completed' AND updated_at < datetime('now','-90 days') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM tasks_archive)
Deletes files in agent-dispatch/output/ matching archived task IDs
Aggregates provider_health records older than 30 days: INSERT INTO health_daily_summary with SUM/AVG, then DELETE raw records
Deletes agent_activity, dispatch_runs, working_memory older than 90 days
Deletes provider_incidents older than 180 days (only if resolved_at < threshold)
Runs as scheduled job (daily heartbeat)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement database size monitoring and vacuum
userStories.description: As the retention system, I want database size monitoring with 500MB alert threshold and PRAGMA incremental_vacuum, falling back to full VACUUM only when no active dispatches, so that database file size stays reasonable. **Implementation:** Function monitor_and_vacuum_db(config, conn) checks DB file size with os.path.getsize(), creates alert notification if > threshold, runs PRAGMA incremental_vacuum if auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL, falls back to VACUUM only when no tasks with dispatch_status='dispatched'.
acceptanceCriteria: Function checks os.path.getsize(db_path) against config max_db_size_mb threshold
Creates alert notification if size exceeds threshold
Runs PRAGMA incremental_vacuum(N) to reclaim space
Checks if auto_vacuum mode is INCREMENTAL via PRAGMA auto_vacuum query
Falls back to full VACUUM only if no tasks with dispatch_status='dispatched' (no active dispatches)
Logs vacuum operation with before/after size
Typecheck passes
